General content – Maintaining UA (updates for corrections or changes from the prior release) and updating overall content not specific to features. CRNs – Formatting, finalizing content for publication, and publishing. Quality assurance and production – Reviews, including formal and technical (DITA XML QA) reviews, and revisions per review findings. Management and localization – Administration, meetings, coordination with GPL Localization on translation work, distribution.

title: 2. Validation Overview > 2.2.4 Installation/Upgrade Testing
text_content: As part of Validation TRR (merge Quote to Cash (Q2C) activities), Product Life Cycle Services (PLCS) will work with Varian Installation Team to perform validation testing of the HAL v4.0 associated Service Technical Instructions (STI’s).  The STI’s are the Installation and Service documentation required to install, upgrade, and maintain the new SW and HW for the device under test. PLCS will work closely with the Installation Team and Global Customer Support to identify candidates to participate in the validation testing of the installation and upgrade documentation. PLCS will use engineering systems and, if available, non-clinical systems to develop the Service Technical Instructions (STI’s) and to perform the validation testing.  Perform installation and upgrades as the User.  Activities performed per appropriate STI’s.  Validate and update STI documentation  Findings are recorded in a Test Report and issues submitted. HAL Treatment Delivery Platform 4.0 Validation Plan

text_content: Scope control will consist of managing requirement changes, enhancements, feature requests and scope changes.  Each will be controlled as outlined below. Requirement changes: If all of these conditions are met:  The changes do not impact the essential functionality and/or user experience of the device  The changed do not impact the program schedule committed to MER by more than two calendar weeks Then the changes will be managed through the document control process using Review Report Forms (LL40). If the above conditions are not all met, then the requirement change is elevated to a change in scope by definition.  See Scope Change section below. Enhancements and Feature Requests: Any requirement changes to implement additional features or enhancements, after Elaboration Phase, will be collected as TFS backlog items reporting to an Enhancement EPIC. title: 1. Introduction > 1.2 Purpose and Scope of the UAP
text_content: The purpose of this UAP is to: Identify deliverables, scope, organization, roles and responsibilities, content strategy and information architecture, managerial process, tasks, schedule, milestones, and technical process required to provide effective documentation for users of the product. Serve as a communication vehicle for the project team and a repository of information for UA tasks. User Assistance Developers (UADs) are part of the project team, attend project meetings, and report status regularly to the project team. The UAP is intended to be kept up to date throughout the project and updated as needed. If key milestones remain unchanged, details in the project plan can be updated without requiring reapproval of this document. Scope of UA updates is set by the trigger for the release (section 1.1).  UA included in this release is listed at section 2. For details on the UA to be created or updated for this release, see section 5, Detailed Project Scope.

title: 4.0 Validation Processes > 4.3 Test Case Management and Approvals
text_content: Test Cases shall be created, reviewed, and approved prior to execution per the Validation Procedure (1), ADLM Guidance (10), and RACI for Design and Development (11). Test records shall be reviewed by a second individual (Validation Manager, Validation Lead, peer Validation Specialist, Product Manager or QA). The approval of the Validation Summary Report/MRD by the Validation representative shall confirm that the test records have received this second review. The documented test process for validation consists of the Test Design Strategy and modular test cases traced to User Needs and acceptance criterion. Selected test cases may trace to more than one user need according to the clinical workflow. Each test case is designed so that the input is targeted at a high-level clinical workflow action and does not state specifically how the user must execute the action. Lastly, users are directed in the user documentation to follow the designated security procedures for properly shutting down the software system, which may include, but are not limited to logging out of the active user account, logging out of the application software, shutting down the device workstation, physically locking the control console, and physically locking the device equipment. source: D68685 HAL 4.0 Cybersecurity Statement Rev 01.docx.pdf
title: 3.0 Definitions > 4.1 Confidentiality
text_content: To ensure that no unauthorized users have access to the data, information, or system structures within the medical device, Varian application software authenticates the user before allowing the user to view and/or utilize the application’s features. Users must enter their unique user ID and password, which is linked to the appropriate user rights for that individual. User rights encompass the user, his/her logon rights, and the system features he/she may access. Access privileges are differentiated based on the assigned user group, which limits system entry to only the trusted users from the appropriate group (e.g., Therapists, Physicists, System Administrators, and Service). For Varian medical devices impacted by cybersecurity concerns, the intended environment of use is a hospital or health care facility with a dedicated and managed computer network infrastructure. title: 3.0 Definitions > 4.2 Integrity > 4.2.2 Software Integrity:
text_content: During the design and development phases of the lifecycle for the application software within Varian medical devices, the integrity of the system build is ensured by controlling the editing process and requiring regular reviews of the code. This is done to ensure that the integrity of the application software is maintained through the construction phase. W hen the software package is ready for release to the user, Varian manufacturing procedures (DND1902) are used to prevent improper or incompatible versions of the software from being distributed or installed. These procedures include measures to ensure the consistency of data, the absence of viruses or malware, and the inclusion of needed user documentation and labeling. For Varian medical devices, installation procedures and updates are handled by trained Varian Service personnel or local Service representatives.
